topic: Article 14-18: Equality, Classification and Affirmative Action
subtopic: Equality before law, equal protection, non-discrimination, reservation, untouchability and titles

content.issueStory: Constitutional equality does not require identical treatment of unequal situations; it forbids arbitrariness and demands that distinctions connect rationally to legitimate constitutional purposes. The equality code spans Articles 14-18. Article 14 permits reasonable classification but rejects class legislation and arbitrariness. Articles 15 and 16 prohibit specified discrimination while enabling affirmative measures. Articles 17 and 18 address untouchability and titles.
content.coreConcept: The equality code spans Articles 14-18. Article 14 permits reasonable classification but rejects class legislation and arbitrariness. Articles 15 and 16 prohibit specified discrimination while enabling affirmative measures. Articles 17 and 18 address untouchability and titles.
dimensions: Article 14 combines equality before law with equal protection of laws and applies to persons.
Reasonable classification requires intelligible differentia and rational nexus with the objective.
Article 15 addresses discrimination and permits specified special provisions for disadvantaged groups.
Article 16 governs equality of opportunity in public employment and constitutionally permitted reservation.
Article 17 abolishes untouchability and supports enforceable penal legislation.
Article 18 abolishes titles subject to constitutional distinctions such as military and academic distinctions.
content.constitutionalLink: Articles 14-18; equality and reservation amendments; E. P. Royappa, Maneka Gandhi, Indra Sawhney and subsequent dated jurisprudence.
content.dataReport: Uploaded source basis: STATIC_09_Polity.pdf and PYQ_06_Polity_Governance.pdf. Refresh amendments, statutory rules, office status and later judgments from official sources: Constitution of India, India Code and Supreme Court judgment database.
content.caseStudy: The move from a narrow classification test toward anti-arbitrariness doctrine made Article 14 a broader check on unreasonable State action.
argumentsFor: Anti-arbitrariness subjects State action to reason.
The equality code links dignity with opportunity.
argumentsAgainst: Reservation design raises identification and distribution questions.
Formal neutrality can preserve structural disadvantage.
content.pyqLink: UPSC patterns test Article-institution matches, exceptions, procedure and comparative statements within Article 14-18: Equality, Classification and Affirmative Action. Resolve each statement through source, scope, procedure and consequence.

answerFramework.intro: Introduce Article 14-18: Equality, Classification and Affirmative Action through the controlling constitutional principle and legal source.
body: Map the authority: Articles 14-18; equality and reservation amendments; E. P. Royappa, Maneka Gandhi, Indra Sawhney and subsequent dated jurisprudence.

Explain the first-order design: Article 14 combines equality before law with equal protection of laws and applies to persons. Reasonable classification requires intelligible differentia and rational nexus with the objective. Article 15 addresses discrimination and permits specified special provisions for disadvantaged groups.

Analyse safeguards and tensions: Article 16 governs equality of opportunity in public employment and constitutionally permitted reservation. Article 17 abolishes untouchability and supports enforceable penal legislation. Article 18 abolishes titles subject to constitutional distinctions such as military and academic distinctions.

Use The move from a narrow classification test toward anti-arbitrariness doctrine made Article 14 a broader check on unreasonable State action. and close with constitutional accountability plus dated legal verification.
answerFramework.conclusion: A strong Polity answer joins constitutional text, institutional purpose, judicial doctrine and a workable accountability safeguard.

mainsExamples: Classification based on relevant risk for regulation.
Reservation in public employment under constitutional conditions.
prelimsFacts: Article 14 applies to persons, not only citizens.
Reasonable classification needs both differentia and nexus.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the controlling rule in Article 14-18: Equality, Classification and Affirmative Action?
revisionBox.answer: The equality code spans Articles 14-18. Article 14 permits reasonable classification but rejects class legislation and arbitrariness. Articles 15 and 16 prohibit specified discrimination while enabling affirmative measures. Articles 17 and 18 address untouchability and titles.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the primary constitutional or legal authority?
revisionBox.answer: Articles 14-18; equality and reservation amendments; E. P. Royappa, Maneka Gandhi, Indra Sawhney and subsequent dated jurisprudence.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the fastest elimination trap?
revisionBox.answer: Do not say Article 14 forbids every classification.

revisionBox.prompt: Where should later legal changes be verified?
revisionBox.answer: Constitution of India, India Code and Supreme Court judgment database

story: Constitutional equality does not require identical treatment of unequal situations; it forbids arbitrariness and demands that distinctions connect rationally to legitimate constitutional purposes.
core_concept: The equality code spans Articles 14-18. Article 14 permits reasonable classification but rejects class legislation and arbitrariness. Articles 15 and 16 prohibit specified discrimination while enabling affirmative measures. Articles 17 and 18 address untouchability and titles.
mnemonic: E-C-A-D: Equality, Classification, Affirmative action, Dignity.

mindmap_json.center: Article 14-18: Equality, Classification and Affirmative Action

branches.label: Text
children: Article 14 combines equality before law with equal protection of laws and applies to persons.
Reasonable classification requires intelligible differentia and rational nexus with the objective.

branches.label: Institution
children: Article 15 addresses discrimination and permits specified special provisions for disadvantaged groups.
Article 16 governs equality of opportunity in public employment and constitutionally permitted reservation.

branches.label: Safeguard
children: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and supports enforceable penal legislation.
Article 18 abolishes titles subject to constitutional distinctions such as military and academic distinctions.

branches.label: Traps
children: Do not say Article 14 forbids every classification.
Do not apply Article 16 to all private employment.
Do not treat affirmative action as an exception wholly outside equality.

key_facts: Article 14 applies to persons, not only citizens.
Reasonable classification needs both differentia and nexus.
common_traps: Do not say Article 14 forbids every classification.
Do not apply Article 16 to all private employment.
Do not treat affirmative action as an exception wholly outside equality.
